cases.question: For active users who have rated at least 3 movies, which films did they give a perfect 5-star rating to? Show each user alongside their personal list of top-rated movies.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (u:User)-[r:RATED]->(m:Movie)
WHERE u.active = true
WITH u, count(r) AS totalRatings, collect(CASE WHEN r.rating = 5 THEN m.title ELSE null END) AS allTopMovies
WHERE totalRatings >= 3
WITH u, [x IN allTopMovies WHERE x IS NOT NULL] AS topRatedMovies
WHERE size(topRatedMovies) > 0
RETURN u.name AS user, topRatedMovies
ORDER BY u.name

cases.question: What movies would you recommend to User 1 based on what similar users enjoyed? Suggest films that User 1 hasn't seen yet but were highly rated by people with overlapping tastes. Show the top 10.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (u1:User {id: $userId})-[r1:RATED]->(m:Movie)<-[r2:RATED]-(similar:User)
WHERE r1.rating >= 3.5 AND r2.rating >= 3.5 AND similar <> u1
WITH u1, similar, count(m) AS overlap
WHERE overlap >= 2
MATCH (similar)-[r3:RATED]->(rec:Movie)
WHERE r3.rating >= 4.0
  AND NOT EXISTS { (u1)-[:RATED]->(rec) }
WITH rec, count(DISTINCT similar) AS recommenders, avg(r3.rating) AS avgRating
RETURN rec.title AS movie,
       recommenders,
       round(avgRating, 2) AS avgRating
ORDER BY recommenders DESC, avgRating DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: Execution error: {neo4j_code: Neo.ClientError.Statement.ParameterMissing} {message: Expected parameter(s): userId} {gql_status: 42001} {gql_status_description: error: syntax error or access rule violation - invalid syntax}

cases.question: What are the top 5 movies I should recommend to User 534? Base it on what users with similar viewing histories rated highly, excluding anything User 534 has already seen.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (target:User {id: 534})-[:RATED]->(seen:Movie)
WITH target, collect(seen) AS seenMovies

MATCH (target)-[:RATED]->(common:Movie)<-[:RATED]-(similar:User)
WHERE similar <> target
WITH target, seenMovies, similar, count(common) AS sharedMovies
ORDER BY sharedMovies DESC
LIMIT 50

MATCH (similar)-[r:RATED]->(candidate:Movie)
WHERE NOT candidate IN seenMovies
  AND r.rating >= 4

WITH candidate, count(DISTINCT similar) AS recommenderCount, avg(r.rating) AS avgRating
ORDER BY recommenderCount DESC, avgRating DESC
LIMIT 5

RETURN candidate.title AS movie,
       recommenderCount,
       round(avgRating * 100) / 100 AS avgRating

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

RETURN p.name AS name, actedCount, directedCount

cases.question: Which genres have the most consistent audience ratings — meaning the least variation between reviewers? Only include genres with at least 50 ratings, and rank from most to least consistent.

WHERE size(ratings) >= 50
WITH genre, stDev([x IN ratings | x]) AS ratingStdDev, size(ratings) AS ratingCount
RETURN genre, ratingStdDev, ratingCount
ORDER BY ratingStdDev ASC

cases.question: What are the top 10 movies to recommend to User 1, factoring in both what similar users enjoyed and whether the movie's genres match their viewing history? Weight peer ratings more heavily than genre overlap.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (targetUser:User {userId: 1})-[r1:RATED]->(m1:Movie)
WITH targetUser, collect(m1) AS watchedMovies, avg(r1.rating) AS avgRating

// Find similar users who rated the same movies highly
MATCH (targetUser)-[r2:RATED]->(commonMovie:Movie)<-[r3:RATED]-(similarUser:User)
WHERE similarUser <> targetUser AND r2.rating >= 3.5 AND r3.rating >= 3.5
WITH targetUser, watchedMovies, similarUser, count(commonMovie) AS commonCount

// Find movies that similar users enjoyed but target user hasn't seen
MATCH (similarUser)-[r4:RATED]->(candidate:Movie)
WHERE NOT candidate IN watchedMovies AND r4.rating >= 4.0
WITH targetUser, watchedMovies, candidate,
     sum(r4.rating * commonCount) / sum(commonCount) AS peerScore,
     count(DISTINCT similarUser) AS peerCount

// Compute genre overlap with user's watch history
MATCH (candidate)-[:IN_GENRE]->(g:Genre)<-[:IN_GENRE]-(watched:Movie)
WHERE watched IN watchedMovies
WITH candidate, peerScore, peerCount,
     count(DISTINCT g) AS genreOverlap

// Score: peer score weighted 0.75, normalized genre overlap weighted 0.25
WITH candidate,
     peerScore,
     genreOverlap,
     (peerScore * 0.75 + toFloat(genreOverlap) * 0.25) AS finalScore

RETURN candidate.title AS title,
       candidate.imdbRating AS imdbRating,
       round(peerScore * 100) / 100 AS peerScore,
       genreOverlap,
       round(finalScore * 100) / 100 AS finalScore
ORDER BY finalScore DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
